title: pseudognaphalium gayanum
intro: Pseudognaphalium gayanum, also known as Gay's cudweed or Gay's rabbit tobacco, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce pain, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Pseudognaphalium gayanum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Pseudognaphalium gayanum is often used in teas, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
